findings.detail: The observe-think-act loop reads clearly as a cycle: USER -> OBS -> THINK -> ACT with a labeled back-edge 'loop: task not done' returning to OBS, and a labeled exit edge 'exit: task done' going to DONE. The cycle is unambiguous — a beginner can trace the loop without any annotation beyond the edge labels already present.

All 7 node labels map directly to corpus Core Concepts: 'User Task' (user gives a goal), 'Observe' (observe step), 'Think' (LLM core reasons), 'Act' (invoke tool or write memory), 'Memory' (short-term + long-term), 'Tools' (web search, file reader, code executor), 'Result Delivered' (deliver result if done). No multi-agent, LangChain, or RAG-internals nodes present. All bold labels <= 4 words. No bare unexplained acronyms as node labels — 'LLM core' appears only in a 12px subtitle span, not as a standalone node label.

Entry (User Task) uses start blue (#a5d8ff). Automated cycle steps (Observe, Think) use auto purple (#d0bfff). Act uses act blue (#a5d8ff), visually distinguishing it as the action-execution node. Memory and Tools use the hitl-adjacent yellow (#fff3bf) to signal they are supporting resources, not primary flow steps. Result Delivered uses done green (#b2f2bb). No color tier violations.
Governing concept 'Agent Architecture — observe, think, act, repeat' is in the config title: field, not a ghost node. Pass.
7 nodes: USER, OBS, THINK, ACT, MEM, TOOLS, DONE. Within the 3-8 range. Pass.

preflight.mermaid_parse: pass (source-level syntax valid; config block correctly structured with title, theme, themeVariables, config.flowchart)
preflight.concept_fidelity: pass (all 7 labels trace to corpus Core Concepts observe-think-act section; no out-of-scope nodes)
preflight.jargon_label: pass (no bare acronyms as node labels; 'LLM core' only in subtitle span)
preflight.title_discipline: pass (governing concept in title: config field, no dangling ghost node)
preflight.single_path: pass (cyclic intent correctly expressed as main path USER->OBS->THINK->ACT with back-edge and exit; side connections MEM<->THINK and TOOLS->ACT are supporting relationships, not branching of a linear concept)

stats.render_note: PNG requires mmdc on PATH. Run: mmdc -i <mmd_path> -o <png_path> -w 1400 -b transparent
